(A) It was a 25-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack
(B) It was a 30-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack
(C) It was a 25-litre North Face Trail Hiker Pack
(D) It was a black 25-litre backpack with no visible branding
(E) It was a 20-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack
explanation: The article states that the FBI analyzed doorbell camera footage and determined the suspect was wearing a "25-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack backpack."
model_output:
The FBI has released surveillance footage showing a masked individual approaching Nancy Guthrie's front door on the night she disappeared. In this footage, the suspect is seen wearing a black Ozark Trail Hiker Pack backpack. ([time.com](https://time.com/7378604/nancy-guthrie-kidnapping-disappearance-suspect-video-reward-savannah/?utm_source=openai))

Based on this information, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 14, 2026. In the aftermath of the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, the RCMP identified the suspect's mother as one of the victims. What was her name?
(A) Shannda Aviugana-Durand
(B) Jennifer Jacobs
(C) Colaina Hall
(D) Sarah Lampert
(E) Mary Simon
explanation: The article identifies the suspect's mother as "Jennifer Jacobs, who was also known as Jennifer Strang."
model_output:
The suspect's mother in the Tumbler Ridge mass shooting was identified as Jennifer Strang, also known as Jennifer Jacobs. She was 39 years old and originally from Lawn, Newfoundland and Labrador. She was found dead at the family home along with her 11-year-old son, Emmett Jacobs. ([au.news.yahoo.com](https://au.news.yahoo.com/shooters-mother-identified-1-8-220113492.html?utm_source=openai))

Among the victims at the school were 12-year-old students Kylie Smith, Abel Mwansa, Zoey Benoit, and Ticaria Lampert; 13-year-old Ezekiel Schofield; and 39-year-old assistant teacher Shannda Aviugana-Durand. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/bef756c3d04d26b76c49a4e2b5154002?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (B) Jennifer Jacobs.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 14, 2026. During the partial government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), what specific reform have Democrats been demanding regarding the appearance of immigration agents?
(A) A requirement for agents to wear body cameras at all times
(B) A mandate for agents to wear high-visibility vests during raids
(C) A requirement for agents to display their full names on their uniforms
(D) A ban on immigration agents wearing face masks
(E) A restriction on agents using unmarked tactical gear
explanation: The article mentions that Democrats have asked for several changes, including "a ban on immigration agents wearing face masks."
model_output:
During the partial government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) in February 2026, Democrats have been demanding that immigration agents display their full names on their uniforms. This demand is part of a broader set of reforms aimed at increasing transparency and accountability in federal immigration enforcement. Other proposed reforms include requiring agents to wear body cameras, banning the use of face masks during operations, and ensuring agents coordinate with state and local law enforcement. ([govexec.com](https://www.govexec.com/management/2026/01/senate-dems-demand-immigration-agents-unmask-wear-body-cameras-and-carry-ids-shutdown-looms/411050/?oref=ge-featured-river-top&utm_source=openai))

The specific reform regarding the display of full names on uniforms is intended to enhance public trust and accountability by making it easier to identify and hold agents responsible for their actions during enforcement activities.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 14, 2026. Former President Barack Obama addressed a racist video shared by Donald Trump's Truth Social account. According to the report, which entity or individual is credited with originally creating the meme in October?
(A) Brian Tyler Cohen
(B) Tim Scott
(C) The White House communications staff
(D) A staff member of the Trump campaign
(E) Xerias
explanation: The article states the clip "appears to have been taken from an X post shared by conservative meme creator Xerias in October."
model_output:
The meme depicting former President Barack Obama and First Lady Michelle Obama as apes was originally created in October 2025 by a conservative meme creator. This AI-generated video portrayed various Democratic figures as animals, with Trump styled as a lion, and was shared on social media platforms, including X (formerly Twitter). ([yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/trump-deletes-racist-video-obamas-185852364.html?utm_source=openai))

In early February 2026, President Donald Trump shared a clip from this video on his Truth Social account, which led to widespread condemnation for its racist imagery. The White House initially defended the post, describing it as part of an internet meme likening Trump to the "King of the Jungle." However, after bipartisan backlash, the video was deleted, and the White House stated that a staffer had "erroneously" made the post. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/a48a6b8884a88f9ec30cd4913e352b51?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the correct answer is (E) Xerias.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 14, 2026. Nikhil Gupta has pleaded guilty to charges related to a plot to kill a Sikh separatist leader in New York. What is the maximum prison sentence he faces for these crimes?
(A) 10 years
(B) 20 years
(C) 25 years
(D) 30 years
(E) 40 years
explanation: The article states: "Nikhil Gupta, 54, admitted to murder-for-hire, conspiracy to commit murder-for-hire, and conspiracy to commit money laundering. He faces up to 40 years in prison."
model_output:
Nikhil Gupta, an Indian national, pleaded guilty to three charges: murder-for-hire, conspiracy to commit murder-for-hire, and conspiracy to commit money laundering. Each charge carries a maximum prison sentence: 10 years for murder-for-hire, 10 years for conspiracy to commit murder-for-hire, and 20 years for conspiracy to commit money laundering. Therefore, the combined maximum potential sentence is 40 years. However, under federal sentencing guidelines, Gupta is expected to receive a sentence between 19½ to 24½ years. ([english.gujaratsamachar.com](https://english.gujaratsamachar.com/news/nri-news/nikhil-gupta-faces-up-to-40-years-in-prison-for-admitting-role-in-ny-plot-74160106803.html?utm_source=openai))

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 14, 2026. US inflation cooled to 2.4% in the 12 months to January. According to the Department of Labor, what was the inflation rate reported for the prior month (December)?
(A) 2.0%
(B) 2.7%
(C) 2.9%
(D) 3.1%
(E) 2.4%
explanation: The article states: "The consumer price index rose by 2.4% over the 12 months to January... That was down from 2.7% in the prior month."
model_output:
According to the U.S. Department of Labor, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for All Urban Consumers increased by 0.3% in December 2025 on a seasonally adjusted basis. Over the 12 months ending December 2025, the all items index rose 2.7% before seasonal adjustment. ([jec.senate.gov](https://www.jec.senate.gov/public/vendor/_accounts/JEC-R/inflation/Monthly%20Inflation%20Update%20%28PDF%29.pdf?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the inflation rate reported for December 2025 was 2.7%.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(A) 150,000
(B) 44,000
(C) 1,200
(D) 2,000
(E) 15,000
explanation: The article states: "Last year the US added an average of just 15,000 jobs a month, very few by historic standards."
model_output:
In 2025, the U.S. economy added a total of 181,000 jobs, averaging approximately 15,000 jobs per month. ([ey.com](https://www.ey.com/en_us/insights/strategy/macroeconomics/employment-report?utm_source=openai)) This figure was significantly lower than the 584,000 jobs added in 2024, which had an average of about 49,000 jobs per month. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/c760d3f86988833bc9ccb3936d44d61b?utm_source=openai))

The slowdown in job growth during 2025 was attributed to several factors, including high interest rates from 2022–2023, federal workforce cutbacks, and erratic trade policies. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/c760d3f86988833bc9ccb3936d44d61b?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the correct answer is (E) 15,000.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

(D) Jesse Van Rootselaar, 18
(E) Justin Van Rootselaar, 39
explanation: The article confirms: "As well as the suspect's mother, 11-year-old stepbrother Emmett Jacobs was also killed, police confirmed."
model_output:
The Tumbler Ridge shooting occurred on February 10, 2026, in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Canada. The suspect, 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar, first killed her mother and 11-year-old stepbrother at their home before attacking the nearby Tumbler Ridge Secondary School. At the school, she killed a teacher and five students aged 12 to 13. Jesse then died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound at the school. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/e577eb5689d1af42f6077a56368d4170?utm_source=openai))

Among the victims, the suspect's 11-year-old stepbrother was identified as Emmett Jacobs. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/e577eb5689d1af42f6077a56368d4170?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 14, 2026. Vice Admiral Thomas Allan warned that the DHS shutdown would affect the US Coast Guard. How many workers did he estimate would go without pay if the agency lacked funding for a few days?
(A) 165,000
(B) 75,000
(C) 43,000
(D) 56,000
(E) 120,000
explanation: The article states: "Vice Admiral Thomas Allan told lawmakers that going a few days without funding would mean about 56,000 workers going without pay..."
model_output:
Vice Admiral Thomas Allan, the U.S. Coast Guard's acting vice commandant, has expressed concerns about the impact of a Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown on Coast Guard personnel. He stated that a shutdown lasting more than a few days would result in personnel working without pay, leading to significant financial hardships and negatively affecting morale. ([stripes.com](https://www.stripes.com/branches/coast_guard/2026-02-11/coast-guard-pay-potential-shutdown-20705210.html?utm_source=openai))

While the exact number of workers affected by the current shutdown is not specified in the available sources, historical data indicates that during previous shutdowns, approximately 41,000 active-duty service members and 2,100 civilians were required to work without pay. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2019/01/23/unacceptable-coast-guards-top-officer-criticizes-lack-payment-government-shutdown/?utm_source=openai))

Given that the Coast Guard's personnel numbers have increased over time, it is reasonable to estimate that a similar or slightly higher number of workers would be affected by the current shutdown.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

(A) 14 January
(B) 14 February
(C) 23 February
(D) 14 December
(E) 30 June
explanation: The article states: "The Department of Homeland Security initially said a federal officer shot a man in the leg after being attacked... on 14 January."
model_output:
The original incident involving the Venezuelan national occurred on January 14, 2026. On that date, Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis was shot in the leg by an ICE agent in Minneapolis. The shooting took place in the north Minneapolis area. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operation_Metro_Surge?utm_source=openai))

Following the incident, federal authorities launched a criminal investigation into two ICE officers for allegedly providing false testimony under oath regarding the shooting. The officers have been placed on administrative leave pending the completion of the investigation. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/13/ice-officers-minneapolis?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (A) 14 January.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 14, 2026. Ilia Malinin landed a backflip at the Milan-Cortina Games, a move that was previously banned. Who was the US champion who last landed a legal backflip at the Olympics in 1976 before the ban was implemented?
(A) Surya Bonaly
(B) Terry Kubicka
(C) Mikhail Shaidorov
(D) Yuma Kagiyama
(E) Shun Sato
explanation: The article states: "In the team event he had also landed the first legal backflip at an Olympic Games since US champion Terry Kubicka in 1976 - after which it was banned for safety reasons."
model_output:
In 1976, American figure skater Terry Kubicka became the first to perform a legal backflip at the Winter Olympics in Innsbruck, Austria. This move was groundbreaking at the time, showcasing his innovative approach to the sport. However, the International Skating Union (ISU) deemed the backflip too dangerous and banned it in 1977, making Kubicka's performance the last legal backflip at the Olympics until its recent reinstatement. ([usfigureskating.org](https://www.usfigureskating.org/news/article/backflip-well-back?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the U.S. champion who last landed a legal backflip at the Olympics in 1976 before the ban was implemented is Terry Kubicka.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(A) Ministry of External Affairs
(B) Department of Justice
(C) Drug Enforcement Administration
(D) Cabinet Secretariat
(E) Ministry of Home Affairs
explanation: The article states: "The officer, Vikash Yadav, worked for Indian government's Cabinet Secretariat, which houses India's foreign intelligence service, the Research and Analysis Wing..."
model_output:
The Research and Analysis Wing (R&AW) is India's primary foreign intelligence agency, operating under the Cabinet Secretariat. The head of R&AW, known as the Secretary (Research), functions within the Cabinet Secretariat, reporting to the Cabinet Secretary and cooperating with the National Security Advisor to advise the Prime Minister on intelligence matters. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Research_%26_Analysis_Wing?utm_source=openai))

In the case of Vikash Yadav, he was reportedly an official of R&AW. ([timesofindia.indiatimes.com](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/hes-been-working-for-nation-no-info-hes-a-spy-vikash-yadav-kin/articleshow/114404983.cms?utm_source=openai))

Given this information, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

model_output:
In April 2025, the Trump administration froze over $2.2 billion in federal grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University. ([hsph.harvard.edu](https://hsph.harvard.edu/news/trump-administration-freezes-2-2-billion-in-grants-to-harvard/?utm_source=openai)) This action was taken after Harvard refused to comply with the administration's demands, including audits of academic programs and changes to the university's governance structure and hiring practices.
